name: LLM Wiki — LinkedIn Pulse Article
noteContentFormat:
> [!info]+ Raw Source — Pending Ingest
> LinkedIn Pulse articles are long-form essays (often thousands of words). Author, subtitle, and reactions are extracted from `{{content}}` during `/ingest`.

> [!note] Known LinkedIn Clipper Gaps
> - `{{author}}` is typically EMPTY for Pulse articles (LinkedIn does not expose author via OG/schema).
> - `{{title}}` may have `(N) ` prefix from browser tab notification counter.
> - `{{published}}` may be a loose string like `April 13` rather than an ISO date.
> - `{{language}}` auto-detection can mislabel Korean articles as `en`.
> - `{{description}}` and `{{image}}` are often empty.
> - Ingest step (`/ingest`) is responsible for cleaning these.

## Original Content

context: LinkedIn Pulse long-form article (/pulse/ URL). Structurally closer to Substack/newsletter than social post. Clipper exposes minimal metadata — content body is rich and preserves headings/code/images, but author/published/description usually require ingest-time extraction.
